item.title: Malaysia’s Anti-Graft Agency to Quiz Ex-Minister Over Arm Deal
item.description: Malaysia’s anti-graft agency said it has summoned former economy minister Rafizi Ramli for questioning following a probe into a deal involving Arm Holdings Plc.

item.title: Vietnam Inflation Quickens as Iran War Raises Energy Costs
item.description: Vietnam’s inflation picked up more than expected in April, as a surge in global energy prices driven by the Iran war began feeding through to transport and input costs.

item.title: Philippines Says Thousands Evacuated as Mayon Volcano Erupts
item.description: The Philippines said on Sunday that thousands of people have evacuated the area south of Manila after Mayon Volcano erupted, and authorities advised the public to stay out of the six-kilometer radius danger zone.

item.title: Japan’s Katayama Declines to Comment on FX Intervention For Now
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama declined to comment if authorities intervened to support the yen last week, after reports said they had entered the market for the first time since 2024.

item.title: Nvidia’s Push Into Physical AI Sparks Rally in Asian Partners
item.description: The list of Asian stocks that benefit from business partnership with Nvidia Corp. is getting longer, as the region further integrates into the AI chip giant’s business ecosystem.

item.title: US Jobs Report to Show Resilience in the Wake of Iran War
item.description: The energy shock from the Iran war may be casting a shadow over the outlook for the US economy, but the labor market is yet to register any signs of it.

item.title: Aegon, Barclays Say Prepare for Market Pain
item.description: Skeptics including Aegon Asset Management and Barclays Plc are getting ready for April’s credit rally to potentially vanish as quickly as it appeared.

item.title: Berkshire Meeting Highlights Tough Balancing Act for Greg Abel
item.description: Greg Abel didn’t take long to address the elephant in the room.

item.title: Cuba’s Remaining Lifelines in Peril as Trump Widens US Sanctions
item.description: Sweeping new US economic sanctions on Cuba could chase international companies off the island as Donald Trump’s administration continues to threaten military force to dislodge the 67-year-old regime.

item.title: Is This a Stock Market Melt-Up? Here Are Ways to Tell
item.description: The stock market is on a tear again, with high-flying technology stocks propelling the S&P 500 Index toward fresh records and luring swaths of momentum-chasing investors. Speculation that the worst of war in Iran is over has traders piling into artificial intelligence darlings such as Broadcom Inc. and Intel Inc. — and sent semiconductor stocks higher in 21 of the last 23 trading sessions.

item.title: OPEC+ Provisionally Agrees June Quota Increase, Delegate Says
item.description: Major OPEC+ nations came to a provisional agreement on another modest but symbolic increase to supply targets for June, in the group’s first move since the UAE’s surprise exit, a delegate said.

item.title: Golden Tempo Takes Kentucky Derby, Makes History for Female Trainer
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Cherie DeVaux spent the week talking a lot about the possibility of becoming the first woman to train a Kentucky Derby winner as she got ready to be just the 18th to enter a horse in the crown jewel of horse racing’s Triple Crown with Golden Tempo.

item.title: Beijing Tells Chinese Firms to Ignore US Sanctions on Refiners
item.description: China ordered companies in the country not to comply with US sanctions on five domestic refiners linked to the Iranian oil trade, in a defiant move aimed at softening the impact of penalties.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp, Jindal Steel Pause Talks on Steel Unit Stake
item.description: Thyssenkrupp AG said it and Jindal Steel International agreed to pause talks about the Indian company acquiring a stake in its steel unit.

item.title: Hungary Warns of Drought Hit to Agriculture From April Dry Spell
item.description: Hungary’s incoming government said it plans to take emergency steps to help farmers as a dry spell during much of April is leading to a severe drought in the grain-producing country.

item.title: Corporate America Earnings Beat Back Wall Street’s Wall of Worry
item.description: First-quarter earnings season is delivering Wall Street better-than-expected results, propelling US equities’ run from one record to the next.

item.title: Iran Juggles Oil Cuts and Storage Strain to Resist US Blockade
item.description: As the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz tightens around Iran’s oil trade, exports have plunged in recent weeks and storage is rapidly filling. Already, the country has begun curbing production, according to a senior Iranian official.

item.title: Kotak CEO Flags IDBI Valuation Concerns as Privatization Drags
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank Ltd.’s chief executive said the valuation sought for a stake in IDBI Bank Ltd. was too high, underscoring tepid bidder appetite for IDBI even as Kotak Mahindra Bank reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings on Saturday.

item.title: Adam ‘Not Satoshi’ Back Is at Peace With Wall Street Crypto Era
item.description: Adam Back has spent weeks being asked yet again, in various forums and by various people, whether he is the inventor of Bitcoin.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway’s Cash Pile Surges to Record $397 Billion
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.’s cash pile jumped to its highest level ever, reaching $397 billion, in Greg Abel’s first quarter as chief executive officer.

item.title: New Rules for India Shadow Banks May Put Tata Sons IPO on Radar
item.description: A tweak by India’s central bank to the definition of shadow lenders has potentially revived the debate about whether Tata Sons Pvt. — the controller of one of the country’s largest conglomerates — could be forced to list in the near future.

item.title: Reinvention at the Fed and in Las Vegas
item.description: The FOMC held its meeting this week.

item.title: Oil Tanker Pricing Feud Embroils a Centuries-Old London Market
item.description: A legal claim from one of the world’s largest oil traders is throwing a spotlight on an arcane but critical corner of global finance — the multibillion-dollar freight market and the 282-year-old City of London institution that sits at the heart of it.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle on Small Output Hike, Reuters Reports
item.description: Seven members of OPEC+ have reached an agreement in principle to raise oil output targets by around 188,000 barrels per day in June, Reuters reported Saturday, citing unnamed sources.

item.title: World’s Largest Container Carrier Plans Route Avoiding Hormuz
item.description: The world’s largest container carrier plans a new service linking Europe with isolated Middle East ports, using trucking across Saudi Arabia and smaller vessels in the Persian Gulf instead of transiting the blocked Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: How Modi Is Ditching Indian Protectionism in Favor of Free Trade
item.description: The prime minister is racking up bilateral deals as he leaves the old ways behind.

item.title: IMF Sees Ivory Coast Growth Easing as Import Costs Drive Deficit
item.description: Ivory Coast’s growth is set to ease as rising import costs widen its external deficit, testing the economy just as the country exits a $4.8 billion International Monetary Fund facility.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: ECB Is Leaning Toward Rate Hikes
item.description: The European Central Bank is leaning toward lifting interest rates as soon as next month unless there are positive developments on energy prices and ending the Iran war.

item.title: Finland Must Continue to Consolidate Public Finances, Rehn Says
item.description: Finland can no longer avoid or delay dealing with its growing public debt, Bank of Finland Governor Olli Rehn said.

item.title: Rehn Says Fast ECB Action Needed If Second-Round Inflation Seen
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council Member Olli Rehn said it’s “clear” that monetary policy must react fast to any signs of inflation becoming entrenched in the economy through higher wages and prices.
